title: Write an immediate assertion for a procedural check
description: Place an immediate assertion in procedural code to check a condition at a specific simulation point.
body: Use when a condition must hold at a precise point in procedural testbench code. Do not use a concurrent assertion for a one-shot procedural check, since the semantics and timing differ. Actions: place the immediate assertion where the condition must hold, attach a clear failure message, and verify it fires when the condition is deliberately broken. Validation signals include the assertion firing on an injected violation and a message that identifies the failing condition. Risk boundary: an immediate assertion in the wrong process can miss the event, so flag any placement where the checked value is not yet stable.
